elements.title: Apumanque Mall
elements.contents: ... that the Apumanque Mall (pictured) had a 70 metre art installation, made out of steel and lighting?

elements.title: Alice Sollier
elements.contents: ... that the Mayor of Paris has suggested adding the name of Alice Sollier, the first Black French woman to qualify as a medical doctor, to the Eiffel Tower?

elements.title: Donuts
elements.contents: ... that J Dilla worked on Donuts, which was released three days before his death, in the hospital?

elements.title: René Chambellant
elements.contents: ... that the 1945–1948 Patriarch of the Universal Gnostic Church René Chambellant later became a Member of Parliament in the Central African Republic?

elements.title: The Inklings
elements.contents: ... that The Inklings suggests that J. R. R. Tolkien was jealous of C. S. Lewis's admiration of Charles Williams?

elements.title: Ahmad Soedarsono
elements.contents: ... that after being asked to resign due to his cancer, ambassador Ahmad Soedarsono responded that he wanted to die on duty?

elements.title: Chinese Australian Herald
elements.contents: ... that the Chinese Australian Herald was distributed as far away as London, the Pacific Islands, and the Philippines?

elements.title: Sachio Ashida
elements.contents: ... that Sachio Ashida rebuilt a guided missile computer and repurposed it for his experiments on brain tissue?

elements.title: refused to play
elements.contents: ... that FC Barcelona refused to play a Copa del Rey game in 2000?
